_align_status_note: mirrored from backend — do not hand-edit here, source of truth is backend/governance/modules/NOTIFICATION/execution-state.json

notes.f1_session1_progress: 2026-07-15 — F1 (XL, chunked by screen per execute-notification.md STEP 0) Session 1/3 done: create-models skill applied to SCR-NOTIF-001 only. Created frontend/src/app/modules/notification/notification-inbox/models/notification-log.model.ts (NotificationLogDto, ContractFilter/ContractSort local pair matching security/user-profiles' precedent, NotificationHistorySearchRequest per api-docs searchHistory.md, and NotificationHistoryFilter — the UI-facing filter panel type F2.md's facade spec already references by name). `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Two judgment calls made, not asked up front: (1) api-docs' NotificationLogResponse uses field `id`, not `notificationLogPk` as F1.md's ENTITY-NOTIF-001 prose says — implemented as `id` per STEP 1.5's api-docs-wins priority and create-models' 'derive from backend response DTOs' rule; F1.md's prose is stale, not a new contract. (2) No notification-log-form.model.ts was created — SCR-NOTIF-001 is PATTERN-3 read+action-only with zero editable fields (F1.md: 'Readonly fields: ALL'), so there is nothing for a FormModel/FormMapper to map; skipped, not forgotten. Also flagging for F4: F4-SCR-NOTIF-001.md's stated paths (`app/features/notifications/...`, `app/shared/components/notification-bell/...`) don't match this repo's actual convention — every real module (security, sales, etc.) lives under `src/app/modules/<domain>/<feature>/`, there is no `app/features/` directory anywhere in this codebase. Placed this session's models under `src/app/modules/notification/notification-inbox/` (matching api-docs' own 'notification-inbox' endpoint group name) since F1 only needs a models/ location, not final routing — F4 will need to reconcile its own path convention against reality when it actually executes, this is not a new decision binding F4. current_phase/current_sub deliberately left at F1/PENDING — Sessions 2 (SCR-NOTIF-002 Template Management) and 3 (SCR-NOTIF-003 Channel Configuration) remain within the same sub, same mid-flight convention as SVCAPI's 4-layer split.
notes.f1_session2_progress: 2026-07-15 — F1 Session 2/3 done: create-models skill applied to SCR-NOTIF-002 (Template Management, PATTERN-1 search+entry). Created frontend/src/app/modules/notification/notification-templates/models/notification-template.model.ts (NotificationTemplateDto, local ContractFilter/ContractSort, NotificationTemplateSearchRequest, Create/UpdateNotificationTemplateRequest per api-docs create.md/update.md/search.md) and notification-template-form.model.ts (NotificationTemplateFormModel + NotificationTemplateFormMapper, 4-method canonical pattern). `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Judgment calls: (1) fileFk excluded from NotificationTemplateDto entirely (present in api-docs response but F1.md explicitly says 'NEVER modeled in TypeScript in Phase 1' — DEFERRED/XM-NOTIF-001, matches intr_complete's confirmation that the mapper never sets it). (2) templateBodyAr/En are optional (`?`) on the Create/UpdateRequest wire types (matches api-docs — RULE-NOTIF-006 bilingual completeness is a service-layer check, not DTO validation, per svcapi_template_bilingual_errcode) but non-optional strings on FormModel (UI-required via Angular Validators, not wire-required). (3) isActiveFl is included in FormModel (F1.md lists it as a REQUIRED form field) but the FormMapper's toCreateRequest/toUpdateRequest both omit it — neither API-NOTIF-007 nor API-NOTIF-008's request body accepts isActiveFl at all; status changes are a separate action (API-NOTIF-009 deactivate, per F2.md's facade spec), not a form field being submitted. Flagged as a genuine F1.md-vs-api-docs form-field gap, not silently resolved. current_phase/current_sub still deliberately at F1/PENDING — Session 3 (SCR-NOTIF-003 Channel Configuration) remains.
notes.f1_complete: 2026-07-15 — F1 Session 3/3 done, phase now fully COMPLETE. create-models applied to SCR-NOTIF-003 (Channel Configuration, PATTERN-2 inline toggle list, 5 fixed rows). Created frontend/src/app/modules/notification/notification-channel-config/models/notification-channel-config.model.ts (NotificationChannelConfigDto, UpdateNotificationChannelConfigRequest — no search/pagination types, API-NOTIF-011 returns a plain array) and notification-channel-config-form.model.ts (NotificationChannelConfigFormModel + a 3-method FormMapper: createEmpty/fromDomain/toUpdateRequest — no toCreateRequest, since API-NOTIF-011/012 are list/update only and this entity has no create endpoint at all, fixed 5 rows). `npx tsc --noEmit` clean, same as Sessions 1/2. Same `id`-not-`<entity>Pk` naming correction as the other two entities (api-docs is source of truth). All 3 screens' models now exist under frontend/src/app/modules/notification/{notification-inbox,notification-templates,notification-channel-config}/models/ — current_phase/current_sub advanced to F2 (Frontend Service Contract Specifications: facades + API services, XL per Weight Map, will need its own chunk plan) per the Exit step in execute-notification.md. Reminder still standing from f1_session1_progress: F4-SCR-NOTIF-00{1,2,3}.md's stated paths don't match this repo's real `src/app/modules/<domain>/<feature>/` convention — not re-litigated per screen, applies to all three.
notes.f2_session1_progress: 2026-07-15 — F2 (XL, chunked by screen same as F1) Session 1/3 done: create-api-service + create-facade applied to SCR-NOTIF-001. Created services/notification-inbox-api.service.ts (searchHistory, plus getUnread/markAsRead as typed contract shells for API-NOTIF-004/005 — not silently omitted per execute-notification.md's constraint, but NOT called from the facade) and facades/notification-inbox.facade.ts (notifications/totalElements/loading/error/filter/currentPage/pageSize + LOV state, matching F2.md's facade spec exactly: Init loads LOV-NOTIF-001/002 and calls API-NOTIF-003, bell/unread-count init step deliberately excluded per DRV-NOTIF-003). Added NotificationUnreadSummary/NotificationSendConfirmation to notification-log.model.ts (deferred API-NOTIF-004/005 response shapes, needed for the API service's typing even though F1 didn't model them). `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Real gap found: F2.md's facade spec says 'LovService.loadOptions(...)' — no such service exists anywhere in this codebase (grepped all of frontend/src/app). The actual, already-existing, providedIn:'root' shared lookup service is LookupService (core/services/lookup.service.ts, getOptions(lookupKey, lang) -> Observable<LookupSelectOption[]>, cached via shareReplay, doc-commented 'All feature modules MUST use this service') — used that instead, same api-docs/reality-over-stale-plan-prose precedent as fileFk/id naming fixes and the earlier GET-vs-POST search-method fixes. No facade in the entire modules/ tree previously injected LookupService — this is the first — so there's no existing precedent for the lang-aware LOV-in-facade pattern used here (TranslateService.currentLang read directly in the facade, matching the one existing LookupService consumer's pattern in finance/gl/accounts-tree.component.ts, just moved into a facade). current_phase/current_sub still deliberately at F2/PENDING — Sessions 2 (SCR-NOTIF-002 Template Management) and 3 (SCR-NOTIF-003 Channel Configuration) remain.
notes.f2_session2_progress: 2026-07-15 — F2 Session 2/3 done: create-api-service + create-facade applied to SCR-NOTIF-002. Created services/notification-template-api.service.ts (search/getById/create/update/deactivate — API-NOTIF-006..010) and facades/notification-template.facade.ts (templates/totalElements/loading/error/saving/saveError/currentTemplate/currentPage/pageSize + channelOptions LOV state via the same LookupService fix as Session 1). `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Design choice: kept separate create()/update() methods rather than F2.md's literal 'save()→API-NOTIF-007/008' single-method phrasing — every existing facade in this codebase (UserProfileFacade, PagesFacade, MasterLookupFacade example) uses separate create/update methods with the choice made at the component/caller level, so this follows established precedent rather than inventing a new merged-method pattern; functionally equivalent, no behavior lost. Also: NotificationTemplateSearchFilter needs no ContractFilter[] conversion (unlike NotificationInboxFacade) — api-docs' NotificationTemplateSearchRequest already exposes templateCode/channelTypeId/moduleCode/isActiveFl as direct named optional properties, not generic filters[] entries, so the facade's search filter maps 1:1 onto the wire request. current_phase/current_sub still deliberately at F2/PENDING — Session 3 (SCR-NOTIF-003 Channel Configuration) remains.
notes.f2_complete: 2026-07-15 — F2 Session 3/3 done, phase now fully COMPLETE. create-api-service + create-facade applied to SCR-NOTIF-003. Created services/notification-channel-config-api.service.ts (listAll/update — API-NOTIF-011/012, no search()) and facades/notification-channel-config.facade.ts (configs/loading/error/savingId/saveError — savingId tracks which single row is mid-save, matching F2.md's 'spinner on the specific row's toggle/save control only' loading-state note; no LOV state, channelTypeId is fixed/read-only). Two thin public methods (toggle(id,isEnabledFl), saveConfig(id,configJson)) both funnel into one private sendUpdate(), matching F2.md's 'toggle()/saveConfig() → API-NOTIF-012' — both preserve the row's other field (toggle keeps existing configJson, saveConfig keeps existing isEnabledFl) since API-NOTIF-012's UpdateRequest requires isEnabledFl. `npx tsc --noEmit` clean, same as all prior sessions. All 3 NOTIFICATION screens now have full services/+facades/ under frontend/src/app/modules/notification/{notification-inbox,notification-templates,notification-channel-config}/ — current_phase/current_sub advanced to F3 (Frontend Validation Rules, XL per Weight Map — ~7 RULE-IDs across the same 3 screens, will need its own chunk plan) per the Exit step in execute-notification.md.
notes.f3_complete: 2026-07-15 — Phase F3 is COMPLETE (single sub, no per-screen chunking needed — Weight Map's XL/'~7 RULE-IDs' estimate did not match reality: F3.md only defines 2 RULE-IDs, RULE-NOTIF-006 bilingual-required and RULE-NOTIF-007 templateCode uniqueness/immutability, plus one cross-screen F3-SEC-RULE-1 permission-field-behavior note). Real discovery this phase: NOTIFICATION has **no Angular components yet** anywhere under frontend/src/app/modules/notification/ (only models/facades/services exist from F1/F2) — per the AMEND-P3-J 12-phase cycle (see [[governance_amend_p3_j_f4_phase]]), routing + components are built in F4 (create-routing + create-components), which comes AFTER F3. This makes F3 a specification-verification phase, same category as DOC/INTC/INTR, not a code-writing phase — there is no reactive form yet to wire Validators.required or disabled-on-edit logic into. Verified F3.md's claims against real backend/frontend code instead: RULE-NOTIF-006/007's ERR-IDs (ERR-NOTIF-0002/0003), exact bilingual EN/AR message text, and DB constraint name (UQ_NOTIF_TEMPLATE_CODE) all match NotificationErrorCodes.java/messages.properties/messages_ar.properties/NotificationTemplate.java verbatim; notification-template-form.model.ts already has templateCode/templateBodyAr/templateBodyEn fields with a comment already citing RULE-NOTIF-007. Zero drift found, no api_doc_gaps entries needed. No backend/frontend code was touched (verification-only), so no validate-backend-feature/validate-frontend-feature run applies — same precedent as doc_complete/intc_complete/intr_complete. current_phase/current_sub advanced to F4 (Frontend Routing & Component Structure, XL — this is where SCR-NOTIF-001/002/003's actual routes/components get built for the first time, and where F3's two validators + F3-SEC-RULE-1's permission-gated field behavior finally get wired into real reactive forms) per the Exit step in execute-notification.md.
notes.f4_complete: 2026-07-15/16 — Phase F4 is COMPLETE, all 3 screens built in one session (chunked internally like F1/F2, no separate state entries). Because NOTIFICATION has no F1-built component shells (unlike SECURITY's pre-AMEND-P3-J F1, which built empty erp-empty-state placeholders — see f3_complete/governance_amend_p3_j_f4_phase memory), F4 was the first phase to write real Angular components for this module: NotificationHistoryComponent (SCR-NOTIF-001, AG Grid + LOV filter panel, read-only, no actions column since mark-as-read is GOVERNANCE-NOTE-BLOCKED), NotificationBellComponent (shared/components, replaces the CodedThemes demo notification dropdown stub previously hardcoded in nav-right.component.html/ts — the fake `notifications` array and its static badge dot were removed), NotificationTemplateSearchComponent+EntryComponent (SCR-NOTIF-002, PATTERN-1 AG Grid + reactive form, edit+deactivate actions), and NotificationChannelConfigComponent (SCR-NOTIF-003, plain Bootstrap table since only 5 fixed rows — no AG Grid needed). Path corrections (same class of fix as f1_session1_progress's flagged F4-SCR-NOTIF-00{1,2,3}.md path mismatch): every F4 sub file said module/component paths live under `app/features/notifications/...`, but that directory doesn't exist anywhere in this codebase — verified against security-routing.module.ts/master-data-routing.module.ts's real convention (`app/modules/<domain>/<feature>/pages/<component>/`) and used that instead; NotificationBellComponent's stated `app/shared/components/notification-bell/` path was already correct (matches file-attachment's precedent). Routing structural decision: NotificationModule is mounted in app-routing.module.ts at `path: ''` (not `path: 'notification'` like security/master-data's prefixed pattern) because F4-RULE-1's routes are absolute top-level paths (/notifications, /notification-templates, /notification-channel-configs) with no shared prefix segment — confirmed by re-reading all 3 F4-SCR-NOTIF-*.md route specs before writing app-routing.module.ts. RULE-NOTIF-006/007 (F3.md) wired as real Angular validators: a `bilingualRequiredValidator` custom function (mirrors RULE-SEC-034's `requiredActiveBranchValidator` precedent) applied to templateBodyAr/En, mapped through form-error-resolver.ts's `bilingualRequiredNotif006` error key to the catalog-exact ERR-NOTIF-0002 text (not generic VALIDATION.REQUIRED); templateCode uses plain Validators.required + is form.disable()'d entirely once in EDIT/VIEW mode (RULE-NOTIF-007 immutability) with server-side 409 duplicate-check surfaced via two new erp-error-mapper.service.ts entries (NOTIF_TEMPLATE_BILINGUAL_REQUIRED, NOTIF_TEMPLATE_CODE_DUPLICATE) added this session — optional client-side ON_BLUR pre-check (F3.md marks it optional, server is authoritative) was skipped, consistent with this session's 'never invent/never over-build beyond what's specified' precedent. F3-SEC-RULE-1 (permission-gated field/button visibility) deliberately NOT wired yet — erpPermission is only applied to actions/buttons that already had an established precedent (create/edit/deactivate/toggle/save), not to blanket-disable whole forms on canEdit=false; that's explicitly SEC phase's job next (permission matrix), matching SECURITY's own F1/F2-then-SEC precedent. Also added: NOTIFICATIONS/NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATES/NOTIFICATION_CHANNEL_CONFIGS i18n sections to both en.json/ar.json (Arabic text for the two new error keys copied verbatim from f3_complete's already-verified backend messages_ar.properties strings). Verified via `npx tsc --noEmit` (clean) AND a full `ng build --configuration=production` (clean — all 3 screens' lazy chunks present: notification-inbox/notification-templates/notification-channel-config, zero new errors, one self-introduced unused-import warning on NotificationBellComponent found and fixed, only the 2 pre-existing unrelated warnings remain). Same Playwright/Chromium environment limitation as F1/F2/F3 (see plan_sec_002_phase3_state memory) — no browser-based visual click-through was possible; a manual verification pass is recommended before trusting this phase as fully proven, especially the bell's live wiring into the global header (touches every page) and the channel-config inline-save flow. PLAYWRIGHT test_phase's gated_by_phases (F1/F2/F3/F4) are now ALL COMPLETE — unblocked and eligible to run any time, same flagging pattern as intr_complete did for JUNIT. current_phase/current_sub advanced to SEC (Permission matrix — @PreAuthorize/service-layer checks, JWT-driven UI gating, and finally F3-SEC-RULE-1's canEdit/canCreate field-and-button visibility) per the Exit step in execute-notification.md.
